items.summary: Commentary: State legislatures weighing in on Workforce Pell New America (blog) With legislative sessions in full swing, 14 state legislatures have introduced bills that affect Workforce Pell implementation. Washington’s co-op preschools face existential crisis after... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Washington Watch: AACC urges administration to reject revised certification for federal grants
items.summary: Along with a series of other associations, the American Association of Community Colleges (AACC) has communicated to the General Services Administration (GSA) its deep concerns about GSA’s proposal to alter the requirements for institutions to... The post Washington Watch: AACC urges administration to reject revised certification for federal grants first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Washington Watch: ED announces SIP eligibility
items.summary: The Trump administration has formally launched the fiscal year (FY) 2026 eligibility process for the Strengthening Institutions Program (SIP) and potentially other Title III programs. The notification should appear in the Federal Register on March 24. ... The post Washington Watch: ED announces SIP eligibility first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: DOJ sues Harvard in bid to recoup federal grants, cut off future access
items.summary: In Friday court filings, the Trump administration again accused the university of failing to protect Jewish and Israeli students from antisemitism and harassment.

items.title: Trump Administration Targets Harvard With Two New Investigations
items.summary: The inquiries target antisemitism and admissions policies. The university called it retaliation for refusing to give in to the administration’s demands.

items.title: Integrating AI Across the Liberal Arts
items.summary: Integrating AI Across the Liberal Arts Joshua.Bay Thu, 03/26/2026 - 03:00 AM The University of Richmond’s new Center for Liberal Arts and AI helps students and faculty explore artificial intelligence ethically, critically and across disciplines. Byline(s) Joshua Bay

items.title: GenAI and Creeping Cognitive Displacement Syndrome
items.summary: As generative artificial intelligence use expands, colleges and universities need clearer ways to recognize when support becomes dependence and how to protect student agency, authorship, and judgment.

items.title: From Prompt to Practice: A Framework for Transparent GenAI Use in Higher Education
items.summary: As generative artificial intelligence reshapes instructional workflows at colleges and universities, a four-level transparency framework can help education developers calibrate documentation and disclosure practices to support ethical responsibility and maintain student trust.

items.title: Lane Community College could cut 20 positions amid looming $4M deficit
items.summary: Leaders at the Oregon institution have also proposed eliminating its health information management and criminal justice programs.

items.title: Trump Administration Begins Investigations Into Three Medical Schools
items.summary: The Justice Department’s demands for admissions-related data from Stanford, Ohio State and the University of California, San Diego, represent a flex of federal power.

items.title: H. Res. 1131 (RH) - Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8029) making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the resolution (H. Res. 1128) expressing the support of the House of Representatives for the Department of Homeland Security; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5103) to establish a program to Beautify the District of Columbia and establish the District of Columbia Safe and Beautiful Commission; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 7084) to amend title 46, United States Code, with respect to the types of vessels that may enter or operate in navigable waters of the United States or transfer cargo in any port or place under the jurisdiction of the United States, and for other purposes; and for other purposes.
items.summary: PDF XML TEXT

items.title: University of North Texas to cut or merge over 70 academic programs
items.summary: The public university is merging two departments and shedding degrees, minors and certificates to address a $45 million deficit.

items.title: Boston University Pulls Pride Flags, Raising Free Speech Worries
items.summary: The university said the flags broke a rule against hanging signs, a policy embraced by other campuses that cracked down on protests. Professors and others say such rules chill speech.

items.title: Hundreds of N.Y.U. Professors Begin Strike Over Pay and Job Protections
items.summary: About 950 full-time faculty members who are not on the tenure track walked out. The university told students that classes would continue.

items.title: Digital accessibility: More than a regulatory obligation

The way each person interacts with the digital... The post Digital accessibility: More than a regulatory obligation first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Ukraine’s Universities Push to Restore Campus Life
items.summary: Ukraine’s Universities Push to Restore Campus Life sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/26/2026 - 03:00 AM Attending in-person lectures is seen as a sign of resilience amid the ongoing conflict that has damaged hundreds of university buildings. Byline(s) Seher Asaf for Times Higher Education

items.title: Power Vacuum at Oxnard College
items.summary: Power Vacuum at Oxnard College Josh Moody Thu, 03/26/2026 - 03:00 AM Byline(s) Josh Moody

items.title: What History, Evidence and Competing Views Tell Us About AI and Higher Education
items.summary: What History, Evidence and Competing Views Tell Us About AI and Higher Education joshua.m.kim@d… Thu, 03/26/2026 - 03:00 AM Conversations about technology and education too often skip past the accumulated evidence on teaching and learning in order to chase what merely feels new. Byline(s) James DeVaney

items.title: Developing the Next Generation of Leaders
items.summary: Higher education leadership development is about intentionally cultivating people, not simply filling vacancies. By investing in mentorship, trust-building, and reflective engagement with younger generations, institutions can build the capacity needed to thrive over time.

items.title: Leveraging storytelling in politics
items.summary: Community college marketing and communications staff play a key role in providing the visuals and storytelling in a college’s efforts to educate and influence lawmakers and other stakeholders. That may seem obvious, but it’s sometimes... The post Leveraging storytelling in politics first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Hampshire College risks losing accreditation amid financial troubles
items.summary: The New England Commission of Higher Education asked the institution to show why it should not be placed on probation or lose accreditation.

items.title: University of Alabama students sue over suspended magazines
items.summary: Plaintiffs say the university’s cancellation of two publications over their focus on women and Black audiences violated the First Amendment.

top_signals.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.core_story: The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This can be framed as an investment comparison: what level of funding is another state prepared to put behind reform, and how does that compare with current commitments in Massachusetts?
top_signals.recommended_angle: Frame this as an investment-gap question for Massachusetts: are leaders expecting community colleges to do system-changing work without system-level funding?

top_signals.title: MassEducate
top_signals.why_it_matters: Useful anchor for Massachusetts affordability policy and how access commitments interact with advising, persistence, and completion capacity.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Useful anchor for Massachusetts affordability policy and how access commitments interact with advising, persistence, and completion capacity.
top_signals.core_story: The core story is whether policy commitments are being matched by the student-support infrastructure needed to improve persistence and completion.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This story may offer a useful comparison point for policy and practice in Massachusetts, especially if leaders are asking whether ambition is matched by implementation capacity.
top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.

briefing_notes.summary_for_brief: Integrating AI Across the Liberal Arts Joshua.Bay Thu, 03/26/2026 - 03:00 AM The University of Richmond’s new Center for Liberal Arts and AI helps students and faculty explore artificial intelligence ethically, critically and across disciplines. Byline(s) Joshua Bay
briefing_notes.core_story: The deeper issue is whether institutions are moving from AI curiosity to real implementation supported by governance, staffing, and strategy.
briefing_notes.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: For Massachusetts, the more important question is not whether AI tools exist, but whether there is meaningful investment in governed, student-centered implementation.
briefing_notes.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.

briefing_notes.title: Developing the Next Generation of Leaders
briefing_notes.summary: Higher education leadership development is about intentionally cultivating people, not simply filling vacancies. By investing in mentorship, trust-building, and reflective engagement with younger generations, institutions can build the capacity needed to thrive over time.

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts policy watch: a comparison worth making
linkedin_drafts.text:
One higher-ed development worth watching this week is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/).

Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What stands out to me is that The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.

For Massachusetts, the key question is whether community colleges are being given the investment, staffing, and policy infrastructure needed to do similar work at scale.

Why this matters: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them. Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

That is where the real policy conversation should begin.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #HigherEdPolicy #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts community college policy watch
linkedin_drafts.text:
One Massachusetts development worth watching this week is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/).

Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What stands out to me is that community college policy is increasingly about more than access alone. The deeper issue is whether institutions have enough advising, transfer, and wraparound-support capacity to convert access into persistence and completion.

Why this matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

For colleges, that is where the real work is.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy

linkedin_drafts.title: Federal higher-ed policy watch
linkedin_drafts.text:
A federal higher-ed development on my radar this week is Headlines (https://www.ccdaily.com/2026/03/headlines-1143/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=headlines-1143).

Commentary: State legislatures weighing in on Workforce Pell New America (blog) With legislative sessions in full swing, 14 state legislatures have introduced bills that affect Workforce Pell implementation. Washington’s co-op preschools face existential crisis after... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

The most important thing about developments like this is not just the policy language itself. It is the operational effect on colleges: advising conversations, aid guidance, workforce programming, reporting obligations, and institutional planning.

Why this matters: Could affect aid eligibility, workforce pathways, grants, reporting, or institutional compliance.

Community colleges are often the institutions that feel these shifts first.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #FinancialAid #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: AI in higher ed: practical implications
linkedin_drafts.text:
One AI-related higher-ed signal I’m tracking this week is MassReconnect (https://www.mass.edu/osfa/programs/massreconnect.asp).

Important Massachusetts reference point for adult student re-engagement, affordability, and the operational demands placed on community colleges.

The real issue for colleges is not whether AI is coming. It is whether institutions can move beyond hype and define useful, governed, student-centered applications. That is especially important in advising, teaching, and student-support settings.

Why this matters: Important Massachusetts reference point for adult student re-engagement, affordability, and the operational demands placed on community colleges.

The colleges that benefit most will likely be the ones that combine experimentation with clear guardrails, staff development, and a strong sense of where human judgment still matters most.

#AIinEducation #HigherEdLeadership #AcademicAdvising #StudentSuccess #EdTech
